objective: Bootstrap the next issue-backed child under issue #615 for fail-closed parity on the root backlog and commit-progression hook path only.
scope_boundary: Owns hooks/backlog-check.sh, hooks/governance-check.sh, the smallest shared helper required for issue-branch backlog/commit-progression parity, focused tests, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local artifacts only.
Must preserve issue #617 as the closed startup/helper child and issue #619 as the separate mutation-time pre-tool lane.

Keeps #607 as the planning-authority dependency, #612 as the degraded-fallback schema and CI enforcement lane, #614 as the downstream consumer verifier lane, and leaves broader hook-stack or CI rewrites for separate lanes.
Starts by producing the issue-local planning packet and validation evidence in this isolated worktree.
out_of_scope: Reopening issue #617 startup or helper surfacing work.
Planning-authority contract rewrites that belong to issue #607.
Execution-scope schema, fallback-log schema, or CI validator changes that belong to issue #612.
Downstream hldpro-sim consumer verifier or drift-gate work that belongs to issue #614.
Broad rewrites of all local hooks or GitHub workflows.
Any claim of repo-wide governance closure.
research_summary: Parent issue #615 and merged children #617 and #619 leave a narrow residual root-hook gap: fail-closed parity between backlog-first authority and commit/branch progression on issue branches. Live source inspection shows backlog-check.sh only runs check_overlord_backlog_github_alignment.py, governance-check.sh validates structured plans, changed-file governance surfaces, backlog alignment, and whitespace, and existing progress staleness tooling explicitly skips hldpro-governance. Specialist review confirmed the next child should stay on hooks/backlog-check.sh, hooks/governance-check.sh, and only the smallest helper required to make standards wording, root-hook behavior, and issue-branch authority lookup agree on one canonical contract.

sprints.name: Packet Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Create the issue-local planning packet and execution-scope boundaries before root-hook parity changes.
tasks: Create the issue-local PDCAR, structured plan, execution scope, handoff, review packet, review placeholders, cross-review note, and validation artifact.
Restate the narrowed root-hook parity surfaces and the live authority contradiction confirmed by specialist review.
Keep the lane in planning_only mode until the packet is validated and alternate-family review is recorded.
acceptance_criteria: The packet names the exact root-hook and helper owned surfaces.
The packet explicitly states the root authority mismatch it must reconcile.
The packet keeps #617, #619, #607, #612, and #614 as explicit external boundaries.
The execution scope allows only issue-local planning artifacts during bootstrap.
The packet states that implementation is blocked until planning validation and alternate-family review complete.
The packet requires a later parity proof matrix rather than generic prose-only closure.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the narrowest valid owned slice under issue #615 after issues #617 and #619 merged.
specialist_reviews.summary: The narrowest valid issue #621 slice is hooks/backlog-check.sh, hooks/governance-check.sh, and only the smallest shared parity helper required for issue-branch backlog/commit-progression parity.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the planning-only bootstrap packet shape and ensure the parity contradiction is explicitly encoded up front.
specialist_reviews.summary: Use issue #619 as the nearest planning-only template, but make issue #621 explicitly prove one canonical authority source instead of leaving split authority between policy wording and root-hook behavior.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude alternate-family review approved the bounded implementation slice with no blocking findings. Residual risk is limited to minor evidence-formatting cleanup around the changed-files placeholder path.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #621 is authorized for the bounded root backlog/commit-progression parity implementation slice on the two root hooks, one smallest branch-issue parity helper, focused tests, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local artifacts only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the bounded root-hook parity fix, return exact blocked and allowed transcripts, co-stage the required governance docs, and close the slice with Stage 6 evidence without widening into #617, #619, #607, #612, or #614.
blocked_on: Issue #617 remains the already-closed startup/helper child and must not be reopened here.
Issue #619 remains the separate mutation-time pre-tool lane and must not be absorbed here.
Issue #607 remains the planning-authority dependency for the parent governance path.
Issue #612 remains the degraded-fallback schema and CI enforcement lane.
Issue #614 remains the downstream hldpro-sim verifier or drift-gate lane.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: AC1: Issue #621 remains bounded to hooks/backlog-check.sh, hooks/governance-check.sh, the smallest parity helper if needed, focused tests, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local artifacts only.
AC2: Standards wording, docs/PROGRESS guidance, root-hook behavior, and helper lookup converge on one canonical governance authority source for this repo.
AC3: Both root hooks fail closed when the current issue branch is missing from the active governance tracker and allow the current issue branch when it is represented there.
AC4: Issue #621 preserves #617, #619, #607, #612, and #614 as external boundaries rather than absorbing their owned surfaces or contracts.
AC5: Implementation proof returns exact blocked and allowed transcripts, focused test evidence, required governance doc co-staging, Stage 6 closeout evidence, and no broader #615 closure claim.

material_deviation_rules: Any edit to .claude/settings.json, hooks/pre-session-context.sh, or scripts/overlord/check_execution_environment.py from issue #621 is a material deviation because those surfaces were already owned by issue #617.

Any edit to hooks/code-write-gate.sh, hooks/schema-guard.sh, or scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py from issue #621 is a material deviation because those belong to the issue #619 mutation-time lane.
Any edit to assert_execution_scope.py, fallback-log schema surfaces, or CI workflow enforcement from issue #621 is a material deviation because those belong to issue #612.
Any implementation of root-hook parity behavior before the issue-local planning packet is validated and alternate-family reviewed is a material deviation.
Any claim that issue #621 closes repo-wide governance enforcement is a material deviation.
